data.title: Trump Adviser and Fundraiser Join Social Media Company Board
data.description: A longtime adviser to U.S. President Donald Trump and a fundraiser involved in soliciting donations for his White House ballroom have been appointed to the board of his social media company. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the strategic move as part of efforts to strengthen the company's leadership. The appointments come amid ongoing developments in the social media landscape, with Trump's platform aiming to expand its influence and reach. The new board members are expected to bring their expertise and connections to support the company's growth and navigate the challenges in the digital space.

data.title: TRUMP Token Plummets Over 93% Amid Controversial Mar-a-Lago Event
data.description:
Up to 297 top holders of the Official Trump token are scheduled to attend a private luncheon with U.S. President Donald Trump at Mar-a-Lago on Saturday. According to NS3.AI, the project has announced that expected attendees include notable figures such as Paolo Ardoino, ChiHyung Song, Anthony Pompliano, and Nathan McCauley. However, Justin Sun's participation has not been publicly confirmed.

The event has sparked criticism from lawmakers and ethics groups, raising concerns about the intersection of politics and cryptocurrency. Meanwhile, the TRUMP token has experienced a significant decline, dropping more than 93% from its initial price of approximately $45 to below $3 since its launch.

data.title: JPMorgan Executive Discusses Future of Tokenization in ETFs
data.description: Tokenization is expected to transform the exchange-traded funds (ETFs) and broader funds industry, according to JPMorgan ETF executive Ciarán Fitzpatrick. According to NS3.AI, Fitzpatrick noted that while strong use cases for tokenization are still a few years away, JPMorgan is actively exploring various applications through Kinexys. The initiative is attracting significant attention from both the industry and regulators as efforts to tokenize equities continue to evolve.

data.title: Brazil Blocks Access to Polymarket and Kalshi, Bans Certain Derivatives
data.description: Brazil has taken decisive action against Polymarket and Kalshi by blocking access to these platforms and prohibiting derivatives linked to sports events, virtual gaming, political outcomes, and other non-economic benchmarks. According to NS3.AI, Finance Minister Dario Durigan stated that these platforms contravene betting regulations sanctioned by Congress and are neither legal nor regulated within the country. Banco Central do Brasil emphasized that this measure aims to safeguard investors and uphold market integrity.

data.title: Trader briefly plunged 80%, currently trading at $1.32.
data.description: Market data shows that TRADOOR plummeted by more than 80% in a short period of time, and is currently trading at $1.32.

data.title: TRADOOR Experiences Sharp Decline, Drops Over 80%
data.description: TRADOOR has experienced a significant drop in its value, plummeting over 80% in a short period. According to Odaily, the current price is reported at $1.32.

data.title: Intel Shares Surge Over 24% Following Strong Earnings and AI Demand
data.description: Intel's stock experienced a significant increase of more than 24%, reaching approximately $83 on April 24, driven by better-than-expected earnings and guidance. According to NS3.AI, the company's first-quarter revenue amounted to $13.58 billion, surpassing the estimated $12.42 billion. The data center and AI segment contributed $5.1 billion to the revenue. Intel has projected its second-quarter revenue to be between $13.8 billion and $14.8 billion, exceeding Wall Street's forecast of $13.07 billion.

data.title: The price difference between APE contracts and spot prices exceeds 10%.
data.description: Market data shows that the price difference between Ape spot and futures contracts exceeds 10%, with spot trading at $0.1806 and futures contracts at $0.16.

data.title: AI TRENDS | U.S. Justice Department Intervenes in xAI's Lawsuit Against Colorado's AI Law
data.description: The U.S. Department of Justice announced its decision on Friday to intervene in xAI's lawsuit challenging Colorado's SB24-205. According to NS3.AI, the law, scheduled to take effect on June 30, mandates that high-risk AI systems used in sectors like hiring, admissions, and mortgage lending must evaluate and mitigate discrimination risks. Additionally, these systems are required to disclose their operational mechanisms and inform consumers when AI influences significant decisions.

data.title: Ape Spot and Futures Price Discrepancy Exceeds 10%
data.description: Market data indicates a significant price discrepancy between Ape spot and futures, with the difference exceeding 10%. According to Odaily, the current spot price is reported at $0.1806, while the futures price is at $0.16.

data.title: South Korean Man Arrested for AI-Generated Wolf Photo Incident
data.description: A 40-year-old man in South Korea has been arrested for sharing an AI-generated image of a wolf named Neukgu, which authorities mistakenly believed to be real. According to NS3.AI, the incident prompted Daejeon city officials to issue an emergency alert to residents. The man faces charges that could result in up to five years of imprisonment or a fine of 10 million Korean won.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | COMEX Silver Futures Speculative Net Long Positions Decline
data.description: The U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) reported a decrease in speculative net long positions for COMEX silver futures. According to Jin10, as of the week ending April 21, these positions fell by 2,184 contracts, reaching a total of 8,863 contracts.

data.title: Upcoming Launch: eCash Hard Fork Set for August Release
data.description: Paul Sztorc has announced plans to introduce a Bitcoin hard fork named eCash in August. According to NS3.AI, Bitcoin holders will receive an equivalent amount of the new eCash coin. The eCash Layer 1 node is designed to closely resemble Bitcoin Core and will utilize the SHA-256d algorithm.

data.title: Iran Denies Requesting Direct Talks with the United States
data.description: Iran has stated that it did not seek direct negotiations with the United States. According to NS3.AI, this clarification comes amid ongoing tensions between the two nations. The Iranian government emphasized that any discussions would need to involve other parties and adhere to previously established frameworks. This statement aims to address speculation about potential diplomatic engagements between Iran and the U.S. in the near future.

data.title: Aave Governance Considers 25,000 ETH Commitment for Kelp DAO Recovery
data.description: Aave's governance is evaluating a proposal to allocate 25,000 ETH to support a recovery plan for Kelp DAO following an exploit of its rsETH bridge. According to NS3.AI, the proposal outlines a remaining funding gap of approximately 75,081 ETH. The plan is currently open for community feedback before proceeding to a Snapshot vote.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Anthropic's Claude Models Achieve High Political Neutrality and Compliance
data.description: Anthropic has announced that its latest Claude models have achieved impressive scores in political neutrality and election policy compliance tests. According to NS3.AI, the models scored between 95% and 96% on political neutrality and 99.8% to 100% on election policy compliance. In preparation for the 2026 U.S. midterm elections, Claude will display election information banners directing users to TurboVote.

data.title: Tennessee bans encrypted ATMs across the board as anti-fraud regulations are tightened.
data.description: Tennessee Governor Bill Lee has signed a bill banning cryptocurrency ATMs statewide, making Tennessee the second state after Indiana to implement a complete ban. The bill (HB 2505) has officially taken effect and will be enforced on July 1st. Under the regulations, installing or operating a "virtual currency self-service terminal" (i.e., a Bitcoin ATM) will be considered a Class A misdemeanor, punishable by up to one year in jail and a $2,500 fine. Merchants who allow such equipment to operate will also face legal liability. Currently, most states in the US have strengthened regulations through licensing systems and transaction limits, but complete bans remain rare. Data shows that since 2026, 30 states have proposed related legislation, with 20 of them having passed laws, demonstrating a continued tightening of controls on the risks of cryptocurrency ATM fraud.

data.title: White House: Investigation into Powell is ongoing
data.description: The White House: The investigation into Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell is ongoing. The case against the Fed is not necessarily going to be dropped.

data.title: Iranian Officials Refute Western Media Reports on Diplomatic Engagements
data.description: Tasnim News Agency has dismissed claims from Western media suggesting that Parliament Speaker Qalibaf would not accompany Foreign Minister Araghchi. According to NS3.AI, Tasnim further clarified that Iranian officials have not engaged in negotiations with the U.S. in Islamabad.

data.title: Brazil Bans Prediction Markets on Elections and Sports Events
data.description: Brazil has implemented a ban on prediction markets related to elections and sporting events, aiming to tighten regulations on this rapidly growing practice. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the global scrutiny these markets are facing. The decision reflects Brazil's efforts to address concerns surrounding the influence and integrity of prediction markets, which have gained popularity worldwide. This move is part of a broader initiative to regulate activities that could impact public perception and decision-making processes. The ban is expected to have significant implications for the industry, as Brazil joins other countries in reevaluating the role of prediction markets in society.
